summary: The posts highlight a range of tactical and technological developments in the UAS conflict. The "Rubikon" Center reported destroying 127 Ukrainian targets on March 25, 2026, across multiple directions, with over 20,000 episodes of enemy target destruction published, 35.4% of which were unmanned systems. The Ukrainian "Vampire" drone bomber, developed by SkyFall, is noted as a mass-produced and effective weapon, while China is developing the ATLAS swarm strike system capable of deploying 96 drones with 3-4 kg warheads over 100 km. On the ground, the "Apachi" unit is actively neutralizing Russian infiltration tactics near Sloviansk, and a Russian Ka-52 helicopter was downed near Nadiivka, Donetsk, with the crew eliminated during evacuation. Discussions also touch on the potential of FPV carriers for kamikaze drones and the limited reusability of the "Yolka" anti-drone system after kinetic interception.
